background: The data below show the percentage of mammal, bird, and reptile/amphibian species at risk (as a percentage of those species known) in eight Central American countries. The data are presented in a table with percentages for each group across countries (Belize, Costa Rica, El Salvador, Guatemala, Honduras, Mexico, Nicaragua, Panama). The goal is to analyze whether there is a difference between the percentage of the different groups of animals (mammals, birds, and reptiles/amphibians) at risk, without considering differences between countries.

task.query: (i) Carry out Friedman's matched group two-way analysis of variance using ranks to determine whether there is a difference between the percentage of the different groups of animals (mammals, birds, and reptiles/amphibians) at risk (assume that we are not interested in differences between countries).
task.answer:
The data below show the percentage of mammal, bird, and reptile/amphibian species at risk (as a percentage of those species known) in eight Central American countries:

\begin{tabular}{lllllllll}
\hline & Belize & Costa Rica & El Salvador & Guatemala & Honduras & Mexico & Nicaragua & Panama \\
\hline Mammals & 7.44 & 4.93 & 5.43 & 5.17 & 4.47 & 7.29 & 5.08 & 6.00 \\
Birds & 0.20 & 0.63 & 0.69 & 1.20 & 0.74 & 12.8 & 0.66 & 0.65 \\
Reptiles/ Amphibians & 6.02 & 2.44 & 5.38 & 3.30 & 4.13 & 3.90 & 4.07 & 3.27 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}

\begin{tabular}{llll}
\hline & Rank mean & \multicolumn{3}{l}{ Friedman's test } \\
\hline Mammals & & $\mathrm{df}:$ & $\mathrm{Fr}:$ & $\mathrm{P}:$ \\
Birds & & & \\
Reptiles/ amphibians & & & \\
\hline
\end{tabular}

task.query: (ii) Using multiple comparison tests where appropriate, what can you conclude from these results?
task.answer: Using multiple comparison tests where appropriate, the results of Friedman's test can be interpreted to determine if there are significant differences between the groups (mammals, birds, and reptiles/amphibians). Specific conclusions depend on the rank means and p-values obtained from the test, which are not provided in the question. Further analysis would involve comparing the rank means to identify which groups differ significantly.
